status: Beta - Full Cycle Master Orchestration Achieved
key_learnings: Architecture: BodCycler_Utils.py is the single source of truth for shared constants (BOD_TYPE, BOD_BOOK_TYPE, BOOK_GUMP_ID, NEXT_PAGE_BTN, CONFIG_FILE, STATS_FILE, INVENTORY_FILE, SUPPLY_FILE) and shared helpers (load_config, check_abort, close_all_gumps, wait_for_gump, wait_for_gump_serial_change, read_stats, write_stats, set_status). All other scripts import from it.

Architecture: BodCycler_NPC_Trade.py has its own local constants block (not in Utils) for NPC-Trade-specific values: NPC_TYPES=[0x0190,0x0191], CTX_BUY=1, CTX_BOD=3, BTN_ACCEPT_BOD=1, BTN_DROP_BOD_1=5, BOD_GUMP_ID_SMALL=0x9bade6ea, BOD_GUMP_ID_LARGE=0xbe0dad1e, CLOTH_1=0x1766, CLOTH_2=0x1767, BOLT_OF_CLOTH_IDS=[0x0F95,0x0F97,0x0F9B,0x0F9C], SCISSORS=0x0F9E, OIL_CLOTH=0x175D, SANDALS=0x170D, BOOK_TYPE=0x2259.
Architecture: BodCycler_NPC_Trade.py reads target_trades and buy_cloth_amount strictly from config (no hardcoded fallbacks) — if missing from config, the function logs an error and returns.

Architecture: Master Cycle order is Crafting → Supplies → Assembly → Trade. Crafting runs FIRST so that BODs are filled before restocking or trading. Supplies runs after to restock for the next round.

Crafting Material Reset: craft_items_until_done must ALWAYS navigate to the Resources tab and set the material on the first item (make_last_ready=False), including mat_btn=6 (Normal). If skipped for Normal, the gump retains the previous BOD's colored material (e.g. Spined), causing Normal BODs to craft with the wrong material. Only 'Make Last' (make_last_ready=True) can safely skip resource navigation, since the material is baked into that action.

Crafting Bug (Fixed): The 'make_last_ready' flag pattern must be used — not 'if attempts > 1'. make_last_ready is set to True only AFTER confirming a new item appeared in the backpack (serial diff before/after). Blindly using Make Last (btn 21) from the 2nd attempt causes the gump to loop without ever navigating to the correct item.

Crafting Bug (Fixed): BOD tooltip cleared after combine — GetTooltip(bod_serial) returns '' immediately after the last item is combined into a BOD. Root cause: Stealth client clears the tooltip cache when the BOD item state changes. is_bod_full() saw amt_to_make=0, returned False, triggering the retry path. Retry immediately found 0 items in backpack (all consumed by the first fill) and returned False again → BOD routed to Riprova. Two-part fix: (1) fill_bod_completely now calls ClickOnObject(bod_serial) + Wait(500) before is_bod_full() to force tooltip repopulation — same pattern as is_item_exceptional(). (2) Retry block in run_crafting_cycle checks FindCount() first: if items are gone (consumed), skip re-filling and do a direct ClickOnObject+Wait(600)+is_bod_full() recheck; if items remain, retry the full fill as before.

Refactor History: The dev branch Utils refactor lost several NPC_Trade constants that were module-level in the original ac5cb99 working-beta commit. When the Utils import was added, the local constants were not preserved. Future refactors must audit all usages before removing local definitions.

BOD Collection (TakeBods): BodCycler_TakeBods.py handles hourly multi-bot BOD collection for collector profiles [ed2, ed3, ed5]. Trigger = hybrid: clock window :55–:05 (server-aligned) AND COLLECTION_COOLDOWN=3300s (55 min) elapsed since last collection. Both gates must pass. last_collection_time is written to stats.json at the end of run_take_bods_cycle(). reset_stats() also stamps it to prevent immediate fire on cycle start.

BOD Collection (TakeBods): The collection cycle is fully event-driven. Connection wait uses _wait_for_connect() polling Connected() for up to 15s instead of fixed sleeps. Gump wait uses wait_for_gump(BOD_GUMP_ID, 10_000) — 10-second timeout, then skips NPC. No hard Wait() for connection or gump.

BOD Collection (TakeBods): Before disconnecting ed4 for the collection handoff, the master cycle walks ed4 to (892, 537) as a standby position. run_take_bods_cycle() reconnects ed4 immediately after ed5 disconnects — no further wait.

BodBookCrate (Origine Replenishment): config key is 'BodBookCrate' (exact casing). When Origine book is empty, _refill_origine_from_book_crate(bbcrate, origine, cycle_type) does a WHOLE-BOOK SWAP: moves depleted Origine into the crate, scans the crate for a BOD book matching the current cycle type (keyword: 'tailor' or 'black' in tooltip) with >50 BODs (parsed via 'deeds in book: N'), moves matching book to backpack, returns new serial. Confirmed tooltip format: 'Bulk Order Book|Blessed|Weight: 1 stone|Deeds in book: 392|Book Name: TAILOR'.

BodBookCrate (Tuple Return): extract_bod_from_origine() returns (0, new_serial) tuple on a book swap, or plain int on success/empty. Call site uses isinstance(result, tuple) to detect a swap: bod, origine = result; if origine==0: break; else continue. The continue re-enters the loop with the updated origine serial so the next BOD is extracted from the new book.

BOD Routing Bug (Fixed): sort_new_bods() in NPC_Trade was routing ALL small non-bone BODs to Origine, including already-FILLED ones. Root cause: no qty_needed==0 check, and consegna_serial was never loaded in that function. Fix: added consegna_serial lookup; in the else branch, qty_needed==0 ALWAYS sends to Consegna (never Conserva — prize routing does not apply to already-filled BODs coming back from trade), qty_needed>0 still goes to Origine.

Stat Ownership: prized_large is incremented in BodCycler_NPC_Trade.py when a Large BOD is routed to Conserva (after sort_new_bods confirms it). prized_small is incremented in BodCycler_Crafting.py when a filled small BOD is routed to Conserva. prizes_dropped incremented in NPC_Trade when a prize lands in the Reward Crate.

Assembly: After run_assembler() completes (success or skip), the master cycle immediately runs BodCycler_Scanner.run_scanner() to rebuild inventory.json from world state. This prevents stale JSON from causing the same set to be found again on the next iteration.

AI Debugger: send_discord_session_report() and call_cloudflare_ai() have been removed. MODEL (DeepSeek) constant removed. Only MODEL_FAST (llama-3.1-8b-instruct-fast) remains. send_error_alert() and send_prize_notification() are the only active Discord functions.
Stealth API: Use FindCount() not GetFindCount()
Stealth API: CloseSimpleGump must be iterated backwards
Gump Parsing: 'Amount' field is plain text (GumpText), others are HTML (XmfHTMLGumpColor)
Parsing Strategy: Group by Y-coordinate (Row) then identify by X-coordinate (Column)
Large BOD Fix: Large BODs may visually report '0' amount; logic must 'steal' correct quantity from child components
Tkinter UI: Tkinter must run in the main thread. Heavy macro logic must run in background threads (daemon=True) to prevent freezing.

Hot-Reloading (Removed): importlib.reload() was previously used before executing scripts from the GUI. Removed in the THR-03 refactor — all modules are now imported once at startup. Hot-reloading introduced race conditions with daemon threads sharing module-level state.
Inter-script Communication: The GUI and worker scripts communicate seamlessly by reading/writing to local JSON files (config, supplies, and stats/status).
Cross-Script Abort: A 'Stopped' status written to JSON by the GUI is polled via check_abort() in inner loops to cleanly halt executing macros.

Trigger Method Stale Status Bug (Fixed): All 5 manual trigger methods in BodCyclerGUI (trigger_trade, trigger_crafting, trigger_supply_check, trigger_scan, trigger_assemble) must call set_global_status('Running (X)') BEFORE launching the daemon thread. If the previous run ended with status 'Stopped' in stats.json and the new thread starts before the status is cleared, check_abort() reads the stale 'Stopped' and halts the worker immediately — the GUI appears to start the action but nothing happens.

Trade Loop — trade_bod Verification: After MoveItem(bod_serial, 1, npc, 0, 0, 0) + Wait(3500), use FindType(BOD_TYPE, Backpack()) + FindCount() > 0 to confirm the BOD actually left the backpack. If still present, log and return False so the trade loop can rotate to the next NPC instead of silently counting a failed trade as a success. Do NOT use FindItem(bod_serial, Backpack()) — FindItem() takes 0 arguments in the Stealth API.

Trade Loop — Multi-NPC Rotation: execute_trade_loop maintains npc_index and consecutive_failures. On each trade, npc_pos = npc_index % len(npc_list). On trade failure: increment consecutive_failures, call sort_new_bods(config) to re-route the dropped BOD, rotate npc_index. If consecutive_failures >= len(npc_list), all NPCs are on cooldown or unreachable — send_error_alert and break. On success: reset consecutive_failures to 0, increment npc_index for natural rotation.

Stop Button Race Condition (Fixed): master_cycle_thread calls set_global_status('Running (X)') at every step. If Stop is clicked mid-step, the worker thread's next set_global_status call overwrites 'Stopped' back to 'Running (X)' — both in the in-memory STATS dict and in the file that check_abort() reads — silently eating the abort signal. Fix: guard at the top of set_global_status: if STATS['status'] == 'Stopped' and 'Running' in status_text: return. Once Stop is signalled, no sub-step status update can clobber it.

Start After Stop Bug (Fixed): Pressing Start after a previous Stop caused master_cycle_thread to log INITIATED then immediately STOPPED. Root cause: the stop guard added to set_global_status (see above) also blocked start_cycling() from setting status to 'Running' — because the guard fires whenever STATS['status'] == 'Stopped' and the new text contains 'Running'. Fix: set_global_status accepts a force=False parameter. start_cycling() calls set_global_status('Running', force=True) to bypass the guard deliberately. Worker sub-step calls never pass force=True, so the guard still protects them.

GUI Layout (Tabbed, XP Style): The BodCyclerGUI was redesigned from a single tall vertical window (~480x980) to a ttk.Notebook tabbed layout (660x470, resizable, minsize 580x430). Theme: xpnative (fallback: winnative), background #ECE9D8, Tahoma font throughout, GROOVE/RAISED relief for frames and buttons. Two internal helpers are defined inside run(): _btn(parent, text, cmd, color=None, **kw) and _lbl(parent, text, textvariable, bold, **kw) — both use props.update(kw) so callers can override any default without a TypeError from duplicate keyword args. Structure: (1) Blue status bar (#003399) at the top with Status label (left) and Courier timer (right). (2) ttk.Notebook with 5 tabs. (3) Bottom control row: Save Config | START CYCLE (dark green #1A7A1A) | STOP (dark red #BB0000).

GUI Tab 1 — Actions: Mode radio (Tailor/Smith), Trades/Cycle entry. Action buttons row: Trade (green), Craft (gold), Scan (pink), Assemble (plum), Check Prizes (light blue), Prize Filter… (salmon). Buy Cloth row: ON/OFF radio + amount entry. Supplies panel: 2-row grid showing Iron/Cloth/Leather (row 0) and Spined/Horned/Barbed (row 1) polled from SUPPLY_FILE. Live Stats LabelFrame: BODs Filled / Small Prizes / Large Prizes / Prizes in a horizontal row, plus Reset Stats and Save Report buttons.

GUI Tab 2 — Books: Lists all 5 BOD book slots (Origine, Conserva, Riprova, Consegna, Scartare). Each row shows current serial in hex (navy) or 'Not Set' (red) and a Target button that calls get_target('books', key, label_widget) to update the serial live.

GUI Tab 3 — Setup: Container targets — MaterialCrate, TrashBarrel, ClothDyeTub, RewardCrate, BodBookCrate — plus RuneBook, all with Target buttons calling get_target(). Home spot row has Set (captures current coords) and Go To buttons.

GUI Tab 4 — Travel: Method radio (Recall / Sacred Journey). Six rune index entries in a 2-column grid: WorkSpot1, WorkSpot2, Tailor1, Tailor2, Smith1, Smith2 — each with a Go button calling test_travel(key).

GUI Tab 5 — Bots: Crafter Profile (single entry, default 'ed4') and Collector Profiles (comma-separated entry, default 'ed2, ed3, ed5'). save_config() splits the comma string into a list; load_config() joins it back for display. Take BODs Now button triggers trigger_take_bods() manually.

GUI Prize Filter Popup: open_prize_filter() opens a Toplevel (300x480, grab_set, resizable vertically). Uses Canvas + ttk.Scrollbar + inner Frame pattern (create_window) for scrollable content. Pre-populates IntVar checkboxes from config['prize_filter']['tailor'] and ['smith']. Tailor section: prize IDs 23 (Clothing Bless Deed) and 24 (Barbed Runic Kit). Smith section: prize IDs 1–22. Save & Close collects checked IDs per group and writes back to self.config['prize_filter'], then calls self.save_config().

BOD Routing Philosophy: Scartare = definitively unwanted (Large BOD with non-prize award, bone material). Riprova = temporarily unprocessable (unknown item name, item_btn not yet mapped in SMITH_ITEMS/TAILOR_ITEMS, missing materials). Unknown items route to Riprova in BOTH sort_new_bods() (NPC_Trade) and run_crafting_cycle() (Crafting) so they surface for manual inspection and dict expansion — never silently discarded.

BOD Routing Bug (Fixed): sort_new_bods() in NPC_Trade checked info.get('item_name') == 'Unknown' (capital U) but parse_bod() always returns 'unknown' (lowercase). The check never matched, so unknown items fell through to the else branch and were routed to Origine instead of Riprova. Fixed to info.get('item_name', '').lower() == 'unknown'.

Smith Gump Structure (UODreams Shard): All metal armor items (ringmail x4, chainmail x3, platemail x6, female plate) are under a single 'Metal Armor' category (cat_btn=1). Items span 2 visual pages but ALL button IDs are accessible with a single category click — no separate Next Page click needed. Page 1 = btns 2-65 (items 1-10), page 2 = btns 72-86 (items 11-13). plate helm is in Helmets category (cat_btn=8, item_btn=30), NOT in Metal Armor. Category buttons follow +7 spacing: Metal Armor=1, Helmets=8, Shields=15, Throwing=22, Bladed=29, Axes=36, Polearms=43, Bashing=50, Cannons=57, Miscellaneous=64. Item buttons within each category also follow +7 spacing starting at 2.

Smith Graphic IDs — Shard Corrections (UODreams): Several standard UO graphic IDs differ on this shard. Verified by BodCycler_SmithIDDebug.py: ringmail sleeves=0x13EF (not 0x13EE), norse helm=0x140E (not 0x1420), bronze shield=0x1B72 (not 0x1B7A), dagger=0x0F51 (not 0x0F52), scimitar=0x13B6 (not 0x1442), viking sword=0x13B9 (not 0x13B0). Also: the shard spells the weapon 'scimitar' (not 'seimitar') in BOD tooltips — the SMITH_ITEMS key must match exactly.
Crafting Reliability: Using a text/Cliloc search within the gump (text_in_gump) is much safer than blind waits and button presses.
Crafting Pacing: Replicating UO Assist/Razor delays -> Click 'Make Last' -> Wait(600) -> Wait dynamically for gump to reappear (up to 5s).
World Save Guard: checkWorldSave.py's world_save_guard() must be called inside all 'Wait()' loops, movement loops, and crafting loops to prevent stalling.
Material IDs & Hues: Cloth uses both 0x1766 and 0x1767. Leather (0x1081) uses specific hues: Normal (0x0000), Spined (0x08AC), Horned (0x0845), Barbed (0x0851).

NPC Context Menus: SetContextMenuHook(npc, id) must be called immediately before RequestContextMenu(npc) with no unrelated code between them. A short Wait(250) between the two is safe and used in request_new_bod for pacing, but longer delays or intervening logic will break the hook registration.
NPC Buying: AutoBuy(Type, Color, Qty) establishes the buy list. Use a list of fallback IDs (e.g., [0x0F95, 0x0F97, 0x0F9B, 0x0F9C]) if shards vary.
Runebook Matrix: Button ID calculation is offset + (rune_index - 1) * 6. (Offset is 5 for Recall, 7 for Sacred Journey).

files.description: Contains definitions for Target Sets, filtered Reward dictionary, and component lists.
files.content: Updated with specific Tailor sets and logic to ignore non-target rewards.

files.description: Smart mapping of item names to (CategoryCraftButton, ItemCraftButton, GraphicID, ResourceCost, MaterialType) for both TAILOR_ITEMS and SMITH_ITEMS. Also contains MATERIAL_MAP.
files.content: SMITH_ITEMS fully populated with confirmed in-game button IDs (UODreams shard). get_craft_info() returns None for any item where cat_btn==0 or item_btn==0 (placeholder sentinel), routing it to Riprova. Graphic IDs verified by BodCycler_SmithIDDebug.py — shard corrections applied (see knowledge entry). Bladed cat=29, Bashing cat=50, Shields cat=15, Helmets cat=8, Metal Armor cat=1.

files.description: Main scanner script. Features 2-pass parsing (Raw -> Final), material inference, and a 'Set Progress Report'.
files.content: Analyzes backpacks to see if full sets can be made.

files.description: The Command Center. Tkinter GUI for setting target serials, saving home coordinates, testing travel routes, and running the Master Cycle.
files.content: Generates config JSONs, reads live stats/supplies, manages hot-reloaded daemon threads, and writes global abort states. Master cycle order: Crafting → Supplies → Assembly (with post-run re-scan) → Trade. BOD collection check uses BodCycler_TakeBods.should_collect_bods() (hybrid clock+cooldown). Before handoff, walks ed4 to (892,537). reset_stats() stamps last_collection_time to prevent immediate fire on start.

files.description: Maintenance script to audit and craft required tools based on 'Tailor' or 'Smith' mode. Restocks ingots and manages tool counts.
files.content: Outputs material counts to supplies JSON for the GUI dashboard. Secured with world_save_guard() and dynamic gump text parsing.

files.description: Intelligently extracts BODs, checks missing quantities, pulls needed materials from the crate, crafts items cleanly, and fills the BOD. Supports both Tailor and Smith cycle_type.
files.content: Imports TAILOR_ITEMS and SMITH_ITEMS from bod_crafting_data. parse_bod(bod_serial, cycle_type) selects correct items_dict by cycle_type. get_craft_info(item_name, cycle_type) returns None if item not in dict OR if cat_btn/item_btn==0 (placeholder) — caller routes to Riprova. Unknown item_name ('unknown') also returns None → Riprova. Smith tool=Tongs(0x0FBC), Tailor tool=Sewing Kit(0x0F9D). Key logic: make_last_ready flag, mat_btn!=6 guard for Resources tab navigation, item serial diff for craft detection, Verifier.test_item_acceptance() for ID mismatch resolution. BodBookCrate: _refill_origine_from_book_crate(bbcrate, origine, cycle_type) does whole-book swap. extract_bod_from_origine returns (0, new_serial) tuple on swap. append_to_inventory() called for all small BODs going to Conserva.

files.description: Helper module imported by BodCycler_Crafting.py to verify crafted items are accepted by BODs.
files.content: test_item_acceptance(): opens BOD gump, presses Combine (ReturnValue==2), targets item, checks tooltip progress delta (retries 3x). get_bod_progress(): parses tooltip to extract current fill count.

files.description: Handles the core trade cycle. Drops Consegna BODs onto Tailor/Smith NPC, accepts new BODs, sorts them, buys cloth bolts (Tailor only), cuts them, and recalls home.
files.content: sort_new_bods(): unknown item_name routes to Riprova (not Scartare). consegna_serial loaded; qty_needed==0 → Consegna. Large BODs: bone or non-prize → Scartare; prize-worthy → Conserva. prized_large incremented when Large BOD enters Conserva. cycle_type-aware: Smith uses Smith1/Smith2 rune keys and find_all_smiths(); cloth buying guarded by cycle_type=='Tailor'. find_smith()/find_all_smiths() search for 'armorer' (preferred) then 'blacksmith' NPC titles. process_prizes_at_home() handles Smith prize types: runic hammers (0x13E3 by hue), power scrolls (0x14F0), ancient smith's hammer (0x0FB4).

files.description: Multi-bot hourly BOD collection. Cycles through collector profiles (ed2, ed3, ed5), collects 1 BOD from Tailor + Blacksmith each, stores them in books, then reconnects ed4.
files.content: should_collect_bods(): clock window :55-:05 AND last_collection_time >= 3300s ago (both gates). _wait_for_connect(): polls Connected() up to 15s. _get_bod(): uses wait_for_gump(10s timeout), skips NPC on timeout. run_take_bods_cycle(): event-driven, writes last_collection_time to stats.json on completion. NPC serials: NPCB=0x0002D23A (Smith), NPCT=0x0002D1D4 (Tailor).

files.description: Standalone automated button mapper for the Blacksmithing gump. Iterates SMITH_CATEGORIES, opens gump per item via text search, writes mapped_smith_items.txt.
files.content: Uses find_button_for_text() (Cliloc then GumpText fallback, nearest button left of text within 20px Y). SMITH_CATEGORIES maps real gump category labels to item lists — 'Metal Armor' is the single category for all ring/chain/plate/female plate. No Next Page click needed; all item buttons (2-93) accessible directly after category click. Run with Tongs in backpack near Anvil/Forge.

files.description: Crafts one of each SMITH_ITEMS entry, captures GetType() on the new item, compares to expected graphic ID, smelts for cleanup, writes smith_id_corrections.json.
files.content: Skips items with item_btn==0. Uses numeric button IDs directly (no text search). Smelt cleanup via btn 14 in the Smith gump. Run with Iron ingots + Tongs near Anvil/Forge. Output: <StealthPath>/Scripts/smith_id_corrections.json.

files.description: Discord + Cloudflare AI integration for error alerts and prize notifications.
files.content: Active functions: send_error_alert() (uses MODEL_FAST=llama-3.1-8b for 1-sentence Discord alerts), send_prize_notification() (instant Discord embed on prize secured), log_failure(), should_trash(), evaluate_riprova_queue(). Removed: MODEL (DeepSeek), call_cloudflare_ai(), send_discord_session_report().

files.description: Scans the Conserva book to identify and build complete Large BOD sets.
files.content: append_to_inventory(bod_obj): appends a BOD entry to inventory.json with pos/drop_btn/page auto-populated. find_completable_sets(inventory): fast JSON-only check for complete sets. run_assembler(): physically verifies and moves BODs in-world.
